intent: Prepend-vs-append ordering (f3 section 2.3, catalog #28). -A prepends a never rule to the FRONT of the list even though it appears on the second text line, so it suppresses an -a always rule listed first. The cost model must order by -A/-a insertion semantics, not text line.

rules.note: PREPENDED (-A) so it sits at the FRONT of the list, before the always rule. Action never => suppressive. Suppresses uid=0 execve. Contributes 0 additive volume.
APPENDED; sits behind the prepended never rule. Net is reduced by the suppressed uid=0 slice (workload-dependent).

cost_band.note: Additive always rule alone; the prepended never (-A) suppresses uid=0, reducing the net. True net <= these numbers.

suspected_trap: A cost model that determines suppression by TEXT-LINE order will see the never rule on line 2 (after the always on line 1) and conclude it does NOT suppress (scenario-33 behavior). But -A PREPENDS (auditctl(8)), so the never rule is actually FIRST in match order and DOES suppress uid=0 execve. The correct net is <= the always rule alone (suppressed uid=0 slice removed); a text-order model over-counts by including the suppressed slice, and an additive-never model over-counts ~2x. The -A vs -a insertion semantics are the load-bearing logic.
